title: Reader Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Static Architecture, and Evidence · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved build trace contains one plausible source, path, encoding, metadata, parser, renderer, template, URL, asset, accessibility, security, cache, package, deployment, or release defect; locate the first failed boundary before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.
Distinguish build-time transformation, immutable output, browser execution, origin hosting, CDN behavior, and external services.
Inspect status, revision, configuration origin, ignores, content licenses, generated files, and existing gates before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Reader Outcome
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug publishing team handles case 1748 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.

Inspect the Reader Outcome trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Reader Outcome.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling. For Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Reader Outcome, keep Reader Outcome connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-evidence-ladder-1sm6x5" that uses case 3713 to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Evidence Ladder.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Evidence Ladder into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Evidence Ladder, keep Evidence Ladder connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-evidence-ladder-1sm6x5
# Competency: ssg-evidence-ladder.
# Case 3713: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_evidence_ladder_1sm6x5(reader_task: str = "find-current-guidance", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, content_authorized: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1sm6x5 = "1sm6x5-5x6ms1-3713"
    assert evidence_variant_1sm6x5
    evidence_axes_1sm6x5 = (
        "reader1sm6x5",
        "source3713",
        "parse5x6ms1",
        "route1sm6x53713",
        "artifact37131sm6x5",
        "recovery5x6ms13713",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1sm6x5)) == 6
    if not reader_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not content_authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "content-authority"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-site-charter"
